title: psephellus phaeopappoides
intro: Psephellus phaeopappoides, also known as the yellow daisy of Crete, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). It is a perennial plant that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been reported to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating digestive issues, making it a valuable resource in herbal medicine. Psephellus phaeopappoides is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally or applied topically to the skin.
